title: crepis coriacea
intro: Crepis coriacea, also known as Rough Hawksbeard or Rough Hawksbit, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Crepis coriacea include alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as supporting liver function and promoting digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its roots and leaves can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
